records.ety: [F. animosit, fr. L. animositas. See Animose, Animate, v. t.]

quotes: Such as give some proof of animosity, audacity, and execution, those she [the crocodile] loveth.

defs: Violent hatred leading to active opposition; active enmity; energetic dislike.
quotes: Such [writings] as naturally conduce to inflame hatreds and make enmities irreconcilable.
[These] factions . . . never suspended their animosities till they ruined that unhappy government.
synonyms: Enmity. Enmity be dormant or concealed

inflamed by collision and mutual injury between opposing parties. The animosities which were continually springing up among the clans in Scotland kept that kingdom in a state of turmoil and bloodshed for successive ages. The animosities which have been engendered among Christian sects have always been the reproach of the church
